description:
You are acting as a senior integration-test developer.  
Inside the workspace you already have a directory

    /home/user/project/api_responses/

containing three JSON response stubs that your backend team generated earlier.  
Your job is to create a **single plain-text listing file** that an automated test runner will later consume.

Required final state after you finish:

1. A new directory must exist:  
       /home/user/project/test/run1/
   (create any missing parent directories).

2. Inside that directory there must be a file named  
       /home/user/project/test/run1/response_files.log

3. The file must list, **one per line**, exactly and only the base-names (no paths) of every “*.json” file currently present in  
       /home/user/project/api_responses/  
   sorted in strict ASCII (byte) order.

4. The listing file must:
   • use UNIX line endings (LF)  
   • contain no leading or trailing spaces on any line  
   • have a single trailing newline after the last entry  
   • contain no other text.

For the current setup the correct final content of “response_files.log” should be

response_200.json
response_400.json
response_500.json

(in that order, followed by one LF).

Important constraints:
• Achieve everything with one shell command (you may chain utilities with pipes or redirections).  
• Do not modify, delete, or move the JSON files themselves.  
• After your command completes, the automated checker will look only at the presence and exact contents of /home/user/project/test/run1/response_files.log.  
• If you need to create directories, ensure they end up with default user-writable permissions.

Produce no additional output files. When you are finished, simply exit the shell; the validator will do the rest.
truth:
Initial (pre-task) filesystem contents:

/home/user/project/api_responses/response_200.json
  {"status":200,"message":"OK"}
/home/user/project/api_responses/response_400.json
  {"status":400,"message":"Bad Request"}
/home/user/project/api_responses/response_500.json
  {"status":500,"message":"Server Error"}

No other files inside /home/user/project/api_responses/.

No directory /home/user/project/test/ exists before the task starts.

Validator expectations after task:

1. Directory /home/user/project/test/run1/ exists.
2. File /home/user/project/test/run1/response_files.log exists.
3. Its exact byte content (shown here with \n representing LF) is:

response_200.json\nresponse_400.json\nresponse_500.json\n

4. No other files are created or modified outside /home/user/project/test/run1/.
